devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । सत्सेवनीयो बत पूरुवंशो । यल्लोकपालो भगवत्प्रधानः । बभूविथेहाजितकीर्तिमालां । पदे पदे नूतनयस्यभीक्ष्णम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca satsevanīyo bata pūruvaṃśo yallokapālo bhagavatpradhānaḥ babhūvithehājitakīrtimālāṃ pade pade nūtanayasyabhīkṣṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
The lineage of Pūru is really worthy of being served by the good, as you, who (as an incarnation of Yama) are one of the Protectors of the world, and who regard the Lord as the chief object, are born in it. At every step and at every moment, you bring freshness to the garland of the glory of the invincible Lord (Hari).

devanagari: सोऽहं नृणां क्षुल्लसुखाय दुःखं । महद्‍गतानां विरमाय तस्य । प्रवर्तये भागवतं पुराणं । यदाह साक्षात् भगवान् ऋषिभ्यः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: so mahad‍gatānāṃ viramāya tasya pravartaye bhāgavataṃ purāṇaṃ yadāha sākṣāt bhagavān ṛṣibhyaḥ
translation: It is for removing the great misery of people who get into it for the sake of petty pleasures, that I commence the Bhāgavata Purāṇa which the venerable Lord narrated to sages.

devanagari: आसीनमुर्व्यां भगवन्तमाद्यं । सङ्कर्षणं देवमकुण्ठसत्त्वम् । विवित्सवस्तत्त्वमतः परस्य । कुमारमुख्या मुनयोऽन्वपृच्छन् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: āsīnamurvyāṃ bhagavantamādyaṃ saṅkarṣaṇaṃ devamakuṇṭhasattvam vivitsavastattvamataḥ parasya kumāramukhyā munayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ad ], [accusative single from √ ad ]

translation: With a desire to know the real nature of the God, higher than Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa, sages, of whom (Sanat-) Kumāra was prominent, asked the respectable Saṅkarṣaṇa, the first of gods, whose ken of knowledge was unobstructed, and who was seated on the ground.

devanagari: स्वमेव धिष्ण्यं बहु मानयन्तं । यद् वासुदेवाभिधमामनन्ति । प्रत्यग्धृताक्षाम्बुजकोशमीषद् । उन्मीलयन्तं विबुधोदयाय ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: svameva dhiṣṇyaṃ bahu mānayantaṃ yad vāsudevābhidhamāmananti pratyagdhṛtākṣāmbujakośamīṣad unmīlayantaṃ vibudhodayāya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: (Saṅkarṣaṇa) who was highly worshipful of his support whom (they)call Vāsudeva, and who had turned unto himself his eyes which were closed like a lotus-bud (while in meditation), and who slightly opened them for favouring wise sages (like Sanaka and others).

devanagari: स्वर्धुन्युदार्द्रैः स्वजटाकलापैः । उपस्पृशन्तश्चरणोपधानम् । पद्मं यदर्चन्त्यहिराजकन्याः । सप्रेम नानाबलिभिर्वरार्थाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: svardhunyudārdraiḥ svajaṭākalāpaiḥ upaspṛśantaścaraṇopadhānam padmaṃ yadarcantyahirājakanyāḥ saprema nānābalibhirvarārthāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: Sages (like Sanatkumāra) touched with their matted hair dripping with the sacred waters of the heavenly Ganges (Mandākinī) the lotus-foot stool of Saṅkarṣaṇa—the foot-stool which the daughters of the king of Snakes worship by offering various gifts with love for getting (good) husbands.

devanagari: मुहुर्गृणन्तो वचसानुराग । स्खलत्पदेनास्य कृतानि तज्ज्ञाः । किरीटसाहस्रमणिप्रवेक । प्रद्योतितोद्दामफणासहस्रम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: muhurgṛṇanto vacasānurāga skhalatpadenāsya kṛtāni tajjñāḥ kirīṭasāhasramaṇipraveka pradyotitoddāmaphaṇāsahasram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ skhal class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ skhal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ skhal class 1 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: (The sages) who knew Saṅkarṣaṇa and (hence) constantly eulogised his deeds in words pronounced falteringly due to intenselove, (asked) Saṅkarṣaṇa whose thousand raised hoods were brightened by excellent precious stones studded in a thousand crowns.

devanagari: प्रोक्तं किलैतद्‍भगवत्तमेन । निवृत्तिधर्माभिरताय तेन । सनत्कुमाराय स चाह पृष्टः । साङ्ख्यायनायाङ्ग धृतव्रताय ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: proktaṃ kilaitad‍bhagavattamena nivṛttidharmābhiratāya tena sanatkumārāya sa cāha pṛṣṭaḥ sāṅkhyāyanāyāṅga dhṛtavratāya
translation: It is traditionally reported that the most venerable Saṅkarṣaṇa taught this to Sanatkumāra who was devoted to the duties of the path of renunciation; and that he (Sanatkumāra), when requested, taught it to Sāṅkhyāyana,[1] the observer of vows.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Sāṅkhyāyana—A sage of the Vasiṣṭha family, probably the same as the author of the Sāṃkhyāyana Brāhmaṇa of the Ṛgveda and. other Śrautasūtras known by his name Bhāratīya Paurāṇika Kośa 341, DHM. 280.

devanagari: साङ्ख्यायनः पारमहंस्यमुख्यो । विवक्षमाणो भगवद्विभूतीः । जगाद सोऽस्मद्‍गुरवेऽन्विताय । पराशरायाथ बृहस्पतेश्च ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sāṅkhyāyanaḥ pāramahaṃsyamukhyo vivakṣamāṇo bhagavadvibhūtīḥ jagāda so parāśarāyātha bṛhaspateśca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: With a desire to extol the powers and glories of the Lord, Sāṅkhyāyana, the foremost among the Paramahaṃsas, narrated it first to his disciple and our preceptor Parāśara and then to Bṛhaspati.

devanagari: प्रोवाच मह्यं स दयालुरुक्तो । मुनिः पुलस्त्येन पुराणमाद्यम् । सोऽहं तवैतत्कथयामि वत्स । श्रद्धालवे नित्यमनुव्रताय ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: provāca mahyaṃ sa dayālurukto muniḥ pulastyena purāṇamādyam so śraddhālave nityamanuvratāya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ ad ], [accusative single from √ ad ]

translation: The merciful sage, blessed by Pulastya[2], expounded to me the first (i.e. the most important) Purāṇa (viz. the Bhāgavata). Oh child (Vidura), I narrate this to you who are faithful, and always devoted (to the Lord).[3]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā records a traditional story: When Parāśara’s father was eaten up by a Rākṣasa, Parāśara wanted to destroy the Rākṣasa race. But Vasiṣṭha dissuaded him. Pulastya finding that his progeny was thus spared, blessed Parāśara that he would be a narrator of Purāṇas.

Footnote [3]:
This tradition of the Bhāgavata is different from that given in the previous Skandhas (vide supra 1.4, II. 9).

devanagari: उदाप्लुतं विश्वमिदं तदासीत् । यन्निद्रयामीलितदृङ् न्यमीलयत् । अहीन्द्रतल्पेऽधिशयान एकः । कृतक्षणः स्वात्मरतौ निरीहः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: udāplutaṃ viśvamidaṃ tadāsīt yannidrayāmīlitadṛṅ nyamīlayat ahīndratalpe kṛtakṣaṇaḥ svātmaratau nirīhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

[nominative dual from √ ram class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ ram class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ ram class 1 verb]

translation: When Viṣṇu delighted in the blissful stage of his own self, inactive and alone, lay on the bed of the king of Snakes (Śeṣa) and closed his eyes in sleep without any interruption of his vision (power of intelligence), this whole universe was immersed in water.

devanagari: सोऽन्तः शरीरेऽर्पितभूतसूक्ष्मः । कालात्मिकां शक्तिमुदीरयाणः । उवास तस्मिन् सलिले पदे स्वे । यथानलो दारुणि रुद्धवीर्यः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: so kālātmikāṃ śaktimudīrayāṇaḥ uvāsa tasmin salile pade sve yathānalo dāruṇi ruddhavīryaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having conserved the subtle elements within his body, and arousing his power called Time, he stayed in the water which was his abode, just like the fire which resides in the wood, without exhibiting his power (of burning).

devanagari: चतुर्युगानां च सहस्रमप्सु । स्वपन् स्वयोदीरितया स्वशक्त्या । कालाख्ययाऽसादितकर्मतन्त्रो । लोकानपीतान्ददृशे स्वदेहे ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: caturyugānāṃ ca sahasramapsu svapan svayodīritayā svaśaktyā kālākhyayā lokānapītāndadṛśe svadehe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ svap class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ svap class 2 verb]

translation: Sleeping over the waters for a period of one thousand aggregates of four yugas, he who had obtained the whole system of doing actions through his power called Time, which was vigilant, visualized all the worlds lying dormant in his own body.

devanagari: तस्यार्थसूक्ष्माभिनिविष्टदृष्टेः । अन्तर्गतोऽर्थो रजसा तनीयान् । गुणेन कालानुगतेन विद्धः । सूष्यंस्तदाभिद्यत नाभिदेशात् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyārthasūkṣmābhiniviṣṭadṛṣṭeḥ antargato guṇena kālānugatena viddhaḥ sūṣyaṃstadābhidyata nābhideśāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vidh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sūṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sūṣ class 1 verb]

translation: Then the (aggregate of) subtle elements lying within him which he viewed intently, got agitated by the attribute called rajas, (which was) impelled by Time. It sprouted forth from the region of his navel, in the process of evolution.

devanagari: स पद्मकोशः सहसोदतिष्ठत् । कालेन कर्मप्रतिबोधनेन । स्वरोचिषा तत्सलिलं विशालं । विद्योतयन्नर्क इवात्मयोनिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa padmakośaḥ sahasodatiṣṭhat kālena karmapratibodhanena svarociṣā tatsalilaṃ viśālaṃ vidyotayannarka ivātmayoniḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: By (the propelling force of) Time which awakens the adṛṣṭa (fate) of beings, the Self-born lotus-bred sprouted forth suddenly, illuminating like the Sun, the vast expanse of water with its splendour.

devanagari: तल्लोकपद्मं स उ एव विष्णुः । प्रावीविशत्सर्वगुणावभासम् । तस्मिन् स्वयं वेदमयो विधाता । स्वयंभुवं यं स्म वदन्ति सोऽभूत् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tallokapadmaṃ sa u eva viṣṇuḥ prāvīviśatsarvaguṇāvabhāsam tasmin svayaṃ vedamayo vidhātā svayaṃbhuvaṃ yaṃ sma vadanti so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ viś class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Vidura, that very Viṣṇu entered (as an antaryāmin) the world-lotus which shows all the guṇas (objects) essential for the enjoyment of jīvas. Within the lotus was born the creator of the world, the Veda incarnate, whom they call self-born (svayambhu).

devanagari: तस्यां स चाम्भोरुहकर्णिकायां । अवस्थितो लोकमपश्यमानः । परिक्रमन् व्योम्नि विवृत्तनेत्रः । चत्वारि लेभेऽनुदिशं मुखानि ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ sa cāmbhoruhakarṇikāyāṃ avasthito lokamapaśyamānaḥ parikraman vyomni vivṛttanetraḥ catvāri lebhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Occupying the stalk of that lotus, and not seeing the worlds, the self-born god Brahmā cast his glances on all sides in the sky, and thus got four faces corresponding to the four directions (of world).

devanagari: तस्माद्युगान्तश्वसनावघूर्ण । जलोर्मिचक्रात् सलिलाद्विरूढम् । उपाश्रितः कञ्जमु लोकतत्त्वं । नात्मानमद्धाविददादिदेवः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādyugāntaśvasanāvaghūrṇa jalormicakrāt salilādvirūḍham upāśritaḥ kañjamu lokatattvaṃ nātmānamaddhāvidadādidevaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: The first god Brahmā, seated upon the lotus which sprang up from the unending high billows of waters, tossed heavily by world-annihilating stormy winds, did not under stand with certainty, the real nature of the lotus which contained, the world, and even of his own self.

devanagari: क एष योऽसौ अहमब्जपृष्ठ । एतत्कुतो वाब्जमनन्यदप्सु । अस्ति ह्यधस्तादिह किञ्चनैतद् । अधिष्ठितं यत्र सता नु भाव्यम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ka eṣa yo etatkuto vābjamananyadapsu asti hyadhastādiha kiñcanaitad adhiṣṭhitaṃ yatra satā nu bhāvyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ bhū ], [accusative single from √ bhū ]

translation: “Who am I seated on this lotus? Whence has this solitary lotus grown on the waters? There must be something beneath, on which this lotus rests supported. That must be surely some positive existence.”

devanagari: स इत्थमुद्वीक्ष्य तदब्जनाल । नाडीभिरन्तर्जलमाविवेश । नार्वाग्गतस्तत् खरनालनाल । नाभिं विचिन्वन् तदविन्दताजः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa itthamudvīkṣya tadabjanāla nāḍībhirantarjalamāviveśa nārvāggatastat kharanālanāla nābhiṃ vicinvan tadavindatājaḥ
translation: Thinking thus, the unborn god (Brahmā) entered through the hollow passage in the stalk of the lotus, into the waters. Going deep down in search of the support of the rough stalk of that lotus, he did not reach it.

devanagari: तमस्यपारे विदुरात्मसर्गं । विचिन्वतोऽभूत् सुमहांस्त्रिणेमिः । यो देहभाजां भयमीरयाणः । परिक्षिणोत्यायुरजस्य हेतिः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tamasyapāre vidurātmasargaṃ vicinvato yo dehabhājāṃ bhayamīrayāṇaḥ parikṣiṇotyāyurajasya hetiḥ
translation: Oh Vidura I A very long time with three tyres (divisions, viz. the past, the present, the future) elapsed while he (Brahmadeva) was searching the source of the lotus (the cause of his creation) in the fathomless darkness—Time which is a weapon in God’s hand which creates terror in beings and reduces (their) life.

devanagari: ततो निवृत्तोऽप्रतिलब्धकामः । स्वधिष्ण्यमासाद्य पुनः स देवः । शनैर्जितश्वासनिवृत्तचित्तो । न्यषीददारूढसमाधियोगः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tato nivṛtto svadhiṣṇyamāsādya punaḥ sa devaḥ śanairjitaśvāsanivṛttacitto nyaṣīdadārūḍhasamādhiyogaḥ
translation: Then the God (Brahmā) who did not achieve his objective, returned thence. Having resumed his seat, he gradually restrained his mind by controlling his breath, and sat down resorting to samādhi-yoga (the path of deep meditation).

devanagari: कालेन सोऽजः पुरुषायुषाभि । प्रवृत्तयोगेन विरूढबोधः । स्वयं तदन्तर्हृदयेऽवभातं । अपश्यतापश्यत यन्न पूर्वम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kālena so pravṛttayogena virūḍhabodhaḥ svayaṃ tadantarhṛdaye apaśyatāpaśyata yanna pūrvam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The unborn God (Brahmā) in whom knowledge arose by the practice of yoga for a period of man’s life-span (100 years), visualized that unforeseen Brahman manifested of its own accord in his heart.

devanagari: मृणालगौरायतशेषभोग । पर्यङ्क एकं पुरुषं शयानम् । फणातपत्रायुतमूर्धरत्‍न । द्युभिर्हतध्वान्तयुगान्ततोये ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛṇālagaurāyataśeṣabhoga paryaṅka ekaṃ puruṣaṃ śayānam phaṇātapatrāyutamūrdharat‍na dyubhirhatadhvāntayugāntatoye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ śī class 2 verb]

translation: He saw a Man (Puruṣa) lying on the bed of the spacious body of Śeṣa white like lotus fibres, on the waters of the deluge, the darkness of which was dispelled by the lustre of gems on the myriad heads of the umbrella-like hoods (of Śeṣa).

devanagari: प्रेक्षां क्षिपन्तं हरितोपलाद्रेः । सन्ध्याभ्रनीवेरु रुरुक्ममूर्ध्नः । रत्‍नोदधारौषधिसौमनस्य । वनस्रजो वेणुभुजाङ्घ्रिपाङ्घ्रेः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: prekṣāṃ kṣipantaṃ haritopalādreḥ sandhyābhranīveru rurukmamūrdhnaḥ rat‍nodadhārauṣadhisaumanasya vanasrajo veṇubhujāṅghripāṅghreḥ
translation: Who excelled in beauty a mountain of emeralds, with (golden) evening clouds overhanging its slopes like garments (which is surpassed by his Pītāmbara—yellow silken garment), with a number of gold peaks (which are outdone in their splendour by his crown), with its Vanamālā—garland of forest flowers—abounding in precious stones, springs, herbs and flowers (excelled in beauty by his Vanamālā), and with bamboos resembling its arms, and trees as its feet (surpassed his arms and feet).

devanagari: आयामतो विस्तरतः स्वमान । देहेन लोकत्रयसङ्ग्रहेण । विचित्रदिव्याभरणांशुकानां । कृतश्रियापाश्रितवेषदेहम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: āyāmato vistarataḥ svamāna dehena lokatrayasaṅgraheṇa vicitradivyābharaṇāṃśukānāṃ kṛtaśriyāpāśritaveṣadeham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]
[nominative single from √ viṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ viṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ viṣ class 1 verb]

translation: Who, by the dimensions of his beautiful and uncomparable body, accommodated the three worlds (viz. Svarga, Mṛtyu and Pātāla), and who (with his body) decorated by a variety of heavenly ornaments and silken garments beautified the ornaments and garments themselves, by his beautiful person.

devanagari: पुंसां स्वकामाय विविक्तमार्गैः । अभ्यर्चतां कामदुघाङ्घ्रिपद्मम् । प्रदर्शयन्तं कृपया नखेन्दु । मयूखभिन्नाङ्गुलिचारुपत्रम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: puṃsāṃ svakāmāya viviktamārgaiḥ abhyarcatāṃ kāmadughāṅghripadmam pradarśayantaṃ kṛpayā nakhendu mayūkhabhinnāṅgulicārupatram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: To men who, for attaining their desired objects, worship him in the pure ways, prescribed in the Vedas, he shows, out of grace, his lotus-like feet with toes beautiful like petals and shining in the rays of his moon-like nails.

devanagari: मुखेन लोकार्तिहरस्मितेन । परिस्फुरत् कुण्डलमण्डितेन । शोणायितेनाधरबिम्बभासा । प्रत्यर्हयन्तं सुनसेन सुभ्र्वा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: mukhena lokārtiharasmitena parisphurat kuṇḍalamaṇḍitena śoṇāyitenādharabimbabhāsā pratyarhayantaṃ sunasena subhrvā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sphur class 6 verb]

translation: Who (reciprocally) honours his devotees by his face with a smile that removes the afflictions of the world, (with ears) beautified by resplendent earrings, (face) appearing reddish on account of his lower lip like bimba fruit, and shapely with its beautiful nose and eyebrows.

devanagari: कदम्बकिञ्जल्कपिशङ्गवाससा । स्वलङ्कृतं मेखलया नितम्बे । हारेण चानन्तधनेन वत्स । श्रीवत्सवक्षःस्थलवल्लभेन ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kadambakiñjalkapiśaṅgavāsasā svalaṅkṛtaṃ mekhalayā nitambe hāreṇa cānantadhanena vatsa śrīvatsavakṣaḥsthalavallabhena
translation: Oh child (Vidura), (he saw the Puruṣa) who appeared beautiful and adorned with a garment, golden in colour, like the pollen of the Kadamba flowers, and with a girdle round his waist, and his chest adorned with an invaluable necklace, and with(his) favourite decoration called Śrīvatsa.

devanagari: परार्ध्यकेयूरमणिप्रवेक । पर्यस्तदोर्दण्डसहस्रशाखम् । अव्यक्तमूलं भुवनाङ्घ्रिपेन्द्र । महीन्द्रभोगैरधिवीतवल्शम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: parārdhyakeyūramaṇipraveka paryastadordaṇḍasahasraśākham avyaktamūlaṃ bhuvanāṅghripendra mahīndrabhogairadhivītavalśam
translation: Who was (like) a great tree of the universe with thousand branches in the forms of arms resplendent with priceless precious stones, and the armlets (keyūra), with its root invisible (in Brahman), and whose arms are coiled round by the body of great snake (or Śeṣa).

devanagari: चराचरौको भगवन् महीध्र । महीन्द्रबन्धुं सलिलोपगूढम् । किरीटसाहस्रहिरण्यश्रृङ्गं । आविर्भवत्कौस्तुभरत्‍नगर्भम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: carācarauko bhagavan mahīdhra mahīndrabandhuṃ salilopagūḍham kirīṭasāhasrahiraṇyaśrṛṅgaṃ āvirbhavatkaustubharat‍nagarbham
translation: Who was like a great mountain, the support of the movables and immovables, a dear friend of the best of snakes (Śeṣa), who is surrounded by waters, whose thousand crowns are like golden peaks, and the gem Kaustubha (adorning) his chest (was like a mine of precious stones).

devanagari: निवीतमाम्नायमधुव्रतश्रिया । स्वकीर्तिमय्या वनमालया हरिम् । सूर्येन्दुवाय्वग्न्यगमं त्रिधामभिः । परिक्रमत् प्राधनिकैर्दुरासदम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: nivītamāmnāyamadhuvrataśriyā svakīrtimayyā vanamālayā harim sūryenduvāyvagnyagamaṃ tridhāmabhiḥ parikramat prādhanikairdurāsadam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]
[nominative single from √ kram class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kram class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kram class 1 verb]

translation: (Brahmā saw) Hari who wore a Vanamālā (garland of Tulasī leaves, etc.) in the form of his glory, (Vanamālā) which beautified by the Veda singing sweetly his glory like humming bees and who is unapproachable to (gods like) the Sun, the Moon, the Wind and the Fire, and who is unassailable due to the weapons with range throughout the three worlds, circling round him.

devanagari: तर्ह्येव तन्नाभिसरःसरोजं । आत्मानमम्भः श्वसनं वियच्च । ददर्श देवो जगतो विधाता । नातः परं लोकविसर्गदृष्टिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tarhyeva tannābhisaraḥsarojaṃ ātmānamambhaḥ śvasanaṃ viyacca dadarśa devo jagato vidhātā nātaḥ paraṃ lokavisargadṛṣṭiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vī class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vī class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: At that very instant (when Brahmā saw Hari), he obtained the knowledge necessary for the creation of the world. He saw the lotus sprouting out of the (Lord’s) pondlike navel, himself, the (cosmic) waters, the Wind (blowing tempestuously during the deluge) and the sky. He saw nothing more (than these).

devanagari: स कर्मबीजं रजसोपरक्तः । प्रजाः सिसृक्षन्नियदेव दृष्ट्वा । अस्तौद् विसर्गाभिमुखस्तमीड्यं । अव्यक्तवर्त्मन्यभिवेशितात्मा ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa karmabījaṃ rajasoparaktaḥ prajāḥ sisṛkṣanniyadeva dṛṣṭvā astaud visargābhimukhastamīḍyaṃ avyaktavartmanyabhiveśitātmā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sṛj ], [vocative single from √ sṛj ]
[nominative single from √ īḍ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ īḍ class 2 verb]

translation:
When he (Brahmā), being associated with the attribute rajas, became desirous of creating beings, and having seen only these (the abovementioned five objects such as the lotus, the cosmic water, the wind, etc.) as the causes of the world, fixed his heart on God and, began to praise the Lord who deserved it.
